summary: This archive is the project-specific continuation and integration package for the Bias Atlas / Psychology of Intelligence Ontology Workbench — a disciplined room for cataloging, arbitrating, and releasing a structured map of cognitive biases that matter to intelligence analysis (Heuer lineage).
final_use_case: It preserves the distinctive design logic for Bias Atlas, so the portfolio can show the actual mechanism and status instead of reducing the project to a file-count or handoff label.

instruments.instrument_name: Bias Atlas Data Schema And Persistence Plan
instruments.input: entities, records, IDs, tables, JSON structures, indexes, and state transitions
instruments.operation: Writing, rhetoric, narrative design, visual culture, and presentation. Use source library writing/visual/cinema sources to strengthen public exposition and Trilogy visual/storytelling logic. Vision: Make public pages read as curated intellectual exhibits rather than dashboards. Mechanism: Extract narrative sequencing, visual perception, close reading, rhetoric, style, cinema, and presentation mechanisms. Plan: Apply after domain-source routing so style clarifies evidence rather than masking weak evidence. Status.

instruments.operation: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Reach for this when: (a) you need a disciplined bias/concept map room pattern (corpus-wall, provenance-spine, release-gate) — the template generalizes to any map workbench; (b) you want an example of the v3.1 Universal Integration Protocol in action with a real 148-entry seed; (c) you are building a Murano room that needs arbitration (merge/split of labels) as a first-class interaction. Entry point: README.md —.
